programme.title: The Last Battle of the Vikings
programme.desc: Nowhere in the British Isles was the Viking connection longer-lasting or deeper than in Scotland. Hundreds of years after their first hit-and-run raids, the Norsemen still dominated huge swathes of the country. But storm clouds were gathering. In 1263 the Norwegian king Haakon IV assembled a fleet of 120 longships to counter Scottish raids on the Norse Hebrides. It was a force comparable in size to the Spanish Armada over three centuries later. But like the Armada, the Norse fleet was eventually defeated by a powerful storm. Driven ashore near present-day Largs, the beleaguered Norsemen were attacked by a Scottish army. The outcome of this vicious encounter would mark the beginning of the end of Norse power in Scotland. Marine archaeologist Dr Jon Henderson tells the incredible story of the Norsemen in Scotland. Visiting fascinating archaeological sites across Scotland and Norway, he reveals that, although the battle at Largs marked the end of an era for the Norsemen, their presence continued to shape the identity and culture of the Scottish nation to the present day.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the role of a few of the millions of insect species, some of which have developed extremely close relationships with plants. Insects pollinate flowers and in some cases neither flower nor insect can survive without the other.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning at London Marylebone, the last great Victorian railway terminus to be built in the capital, Michael Portillo embarks on a postwar exploration of Britain’s southern counties. He travels to Denham in Buckinghamshire, where engineers are constructing a striking new viaduct, High Wycombe, a historic centre of furniture-making thanks to its abundant of beechwood forest, and Oxford, where he discovers the postwar origins of the charity shop. In the Wiltshire countryside, Michael meets the head of the Soil Association and some of her saddleback piglets to hear how the organic movement was founded after the Second World War in reaction to the intensification of farming.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: From the 2016 BBC Proms, Martha Argerich performs Schubert's Rondo in A.

programme.title: Summer Night Concert from Vienna
programme.desc: The Vienna Philharmonic once again take up residency at the Schönbrunn Palace to bring us their annual Summer Night Concert. Set in the picturesque gardens, this concert is a staple of the classical music calendar. For the first time, the orchestra welcomes rising star Lorenzo Viotti as guest conductor – one of the most exciting young conductors on the scene. Joining them for this special evening is Welsh bass-baritone Sir Bryn Terfel, whose relationship with the orchestra spans many decades. He brings us arias from Verdi’s Falstaff, Wagner’s Das Rheingold along with Tevye’s song from the musical Fiddler on the Roof. Projected onto the façade of the Schönbrunn Palace, dancers from the Vienna State Ballet join the orchestra to bring the story of Daphnis and Chloé to life through the power of Ravel’s music. With music from the worlds of opera, musicals and ballet, together with symphonic works, this concert is a joyous musical summer treat.

programme.title: The Lark Ascending
programme.desc: Dame Diana Rigg explores the enduring popularity of The Lark Ascending by the English composer Ralph Vaughan Williams, which was recently voted Britain's favourite piece of classical music by listeners to Desert Island Discs on BBC Radio 4. Composed at a key turning point in world history, The Lark Ascending represents music for all occasions. It is used in rites of passage such as births, deaths and marriages, and is a favourite for film-makers looking to create that quintessential English pastoral feel. Fans of the work include actor Peter Sallis, who wants a copy of The Lark Ascending to be buried with him, top violinist Tasmin Little, who has played the piece as part of the BBC Proms, and music critic Michael Kennedy, who was a personal friend of Vaughan Williams. The programme includes a beautiful new performance of the work in the same village hall where it was heard for the first time in December 1920. The Lark Ascending is performed by 15-year-old violin prodigy Julia Hwang and pianist Charles Matthews, using the original arrangement for violin and piano.

programme.title: Letters from Baghdad
programme.desc: The extraordinary and dramatic story of Gertrude Bell, the most powerful woman in the British Empire in her day. She shaped the modern Middle East after World War I in ways that still reverberate today. More influential than her friend and colleague Lawrence of Arabia, Bell helped draw the borders of Iraq and established the Iraq Museum. Using never-seen-before footage of the region, the film chronicles Bell's extraordinary journey into both the uncharted Arabian desert and the inner sanctum of British male colonial power. With unique access to documents from the Iraq National Library and Archive and Gertrude Bell's own 1,600 letters, the story is told entirely in the words of the players of the day, excerpted verbatim from intimate letters, private diaries and secret communiques. It is a unique look at both a remarkable woman and the tangled history of Iraq.

programme.title: The Man Who Discovered Egypt
programme.desc: Documentary about English Egyptologist Flinders Petrie, the pioneer of systematic methodology in archaeology. Ancient Egypt was vandalised by tomb raiders and treasure hunters until this Victorian adventurer took them on. Most people have never heard of him, but this maverick undertook a scientific survey of the pyramids, discovered the oldest portraits in the world, unearthed Egypt's prehistoric roots - and in the process invented modern field archaeology, giving meaning to a whole civilisation.

programme.title: Summer Night Concert from Vienna
programme.desc: The Vienna Philharmonic once again take up residency at the Schönbrunn Palace to bring us their annual Summer Night Concert. Set in the picturesque gardens, this concert is a staple of the classical music calendar. The Lark Ascending is performed by 15-year-old violin prodigy Julia Hwang and pianist Charles Matthews, using the original arrangement for violin and piano.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins Navy Wings pilots for a spectacular close formation flight in the skies over Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. He also visits the magnificent Elizabethan mansion of Longleat, seat of the Marquesses of Bath, to find out about the estate’s remarkable survival after the Second World War. At Chippenham in Wiltshire, Michael pays a call on a signalling manufacturer that has been at the heart of British railway projects for decades and has a go on the centre’s simulator, driving the Elizabeth Line train to Paddington.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches England’s south coast to continue his exploration of postwar Britain. He begins in Dorset on the Swanage Railway, riding a glorious 1940s steam locomotive on the trail of one of Britain’s most popular children’s authors, Enid Blyton. In Poole, Michael discovers an icon of the Swinging Sixties – the lava lamp, invented in the town by an eccentric accountant. At Romsey, Michael heads to the magnificent Broadlands Estate, once home of Louis Mountbatten, first Earl Mountbatten of Burma. Michael’s last stop on this leg of the journey is Portchester, on the northern edge of Portsmouth Harbour, where he visits a factory manufacturing a marine craft pioneered in the 1950s – the hovercraft.

programme.title: Wild
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan explores his native Mull and some of the nearby islands, filming otters, deer, puffins, seals and a minke whale.

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke explores the surprising roots of Greek art, beginning his journey in Crete at the palace of Knossos, legendary home of the Minotaur. He travels to Santorini to the 'Greek Pompeii' and finds gold in the fabled stronghold of Mycenae and dazzling remains from Greece's Dark Ages. Alastair discovers the beginnings of a defining spirit in Greek art, embracing mythology, a passion for symmetry and an obsession with the human body.

programme.title: Andy Warhol's America
programme.desc: The final episode sees a much more cautious Warhol: a man obsessed with money and security as he reflects on the upper echelons of American society, drag queens and racism.

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Could alien life exist closer than we think - perhaps right next door? The team investigate evidence that suggests the search for life beyond Earth may lie within our own solar system.

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes focuses on work that responded to the Vietnam War and civil strife. He traces the development of minimalism and ends up on top of an extinct volcano.

programme.title: Stolen: Catching the Art Thieves
programme.desc: This episode looks at the theft of a self-portrait of Rembrandt in a brutal robbery in Stockholm and hears from the investigators leading a complex hunt from Russian organised crime to America. The investigation takes a surprising twist involving charismatic FBI agents operating in Hollywood.

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece

Alastair discovers the beginnings of a defining spirit in Greek art, embracing mythology, a passion for symmetry and an obsession with the human body.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Havant, a town that rapidly expanded after the Second World War and attracted new manufacturing, including a factory making a new and exciting toy – Scalextric. Further north, at Rowlands Castle, Michael visits Britain’s oldest commercial vineyard, Hambledon, established in 1952. Crossing the county border into Surrey, Michael heads to Haslemere, the surprise birthplace of a musical phenomenon – the plastic recorder. And the last stop on this leg of Michael’s journey is Guildford, where he discovers a cutting-edge facility at the forefront of the British space industry.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Jump on board with narrowboater Robbie Cumming as he explores the Victorian village of Saltaire in West Yorkshire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal, moors up in Leeds Dock, and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation.

programme.title: Remembers...
programme.desc: Penelope Keith casts an affectionate eye back on the much-loved sitcom To the Manor Born and her role as upper-class Audrey fforbes-Hamilton, who finds herself down on her luck and forced to change her circumstances and home after the death of her husband. Penelope tells the story behind how the comedy came into being, what it was like working with fellow cast members Angela Thorne and Peter Bowles, and the challenges she faced taking on a new role after the huge success she’d enjoyed playing Margo in The Good Life.

programme.title: To the Manor Born
programme.desc: Stately sitcom. When DeVere decides to film a commercial at the manor, Audrey thinks the world should know the truth about him - and about her.

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Tom finds marketing his garden produce more difficult than he thought, especially when the rates are due.

programme.title: Hay Fever
programme.desc: The members of the bohemian Bliss family each, independently, invite a guest up for a very chaotic weekend. Play by Noel Coward.

programme.title: Corridors of Power: Should America Police the World?
programme.desc: After the Islamic revolution and the capture of 50 American hostages, Iran became a bitter enemy of the United States. To counterbalance Iranian influence in the region, the White House turned to Saddam Hussein’s Iraq. US support for Iraq during the Iran-Iraq war ignored massacres, such as widespread use of chemical weapons against both the Iranians and the Kurdish ethnic minority in Iraq, as Washington maintained its close relationship with Baghdad. But with the end of the Cold War, Saddam Hussein’s invasion of Kuwait turned his American ally into an enemy. Would the US now intervene to help the Kurds?

programme.title: Corridors of Power: Should America Police the World?
programme.desc: After the fall of the Berlin Wall, President George HW Bush declared the age of a new world order, where the 'rule of law supplants the rule of the jungle'. For a new generation of leaders, the west’s victory came with a responsibility – to use force to make the world a better place. Bosnia was their first test. Communism had held the disparate ethnic communities in the Balkans together as Yugoslavia, but following the seismic political changes in eastern Europe, the country deteriorated into a series of bloody wars. Should the incoming young President Bill Clinton and the then US ambassador to the UN Madeleine Albright use force to save innocent civilians from a genocide whose brutality and horror was being captured on the home movie cameras of its perpetrators?

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes focuses on work that responded to the Vietnam War and civil strife. He traces the development of minimalism and ends up on top of an extinct volcano.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: TechXplore
programme.desc: As football fever sweeps the globe, Paul Carter discovers how technology is transforming the beautiful game and leaving a legacy for future generations. Kicking off at Boston Dynamics, Paul meets the robots redefining matchday and goes up against 'robot Ronaldo'. In Philadelphia, he visits the university students using AI to unlock new levels of sporting performance. And at TOCA in California, he puts cutting-edge ball technology to the test, alongside the stars of tomorrow. From artists to innovators, join Paul as he meets the pioneering people shaping the future of football.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: What Happened at...?
programme.desc: On 1 July 1916, nearly 20,000 British soldiers were killed on the first day of the Battle of the Somme – one of the bloodiest days in the history of warfare and a lasting symbol of both sacrifice and futility. In this documentary, Jordan Dunbar examines how communities have been shaped by the battle and reveals how its legacy continues to resonate through families - including his own.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Clive Myrie’s Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive visits the bustling district of Vedado, the most modern and affluent part of Havana. He takes a motorcycle tour with Ernesto Guevara, Che Guevara’s son, finds out how to create an amazing feast even though Cubans live under rationing, and stretches himself with a lesson in ballet at Carlos Acosta’s school. He also learns how to make the perfect Cuban cocktail.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.desc: A global programme that reflects the variety of takes on climate change, how best to understand it and the world's attempts to avert it, temper it or adapt to it. It is not about questioning whether climate change is happening, it's about finding the best ways to respond to it. This is sharp-edged, analytical inquiry. Hard scrutiny, touched with a sense of adventure and discovery, and where we can find it, hope. It includes stories from across the world on why we find it so hard to save our own planet, and how we might change that.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Dragon is the ultimate symbol of China. Chinese people often call themselves the descendants of the dragon. Unlike in western mythology where dragons are depicted as monsters to be tamed, in China, dragons are seen as life-giving, beneficent and auspicious. Once you know about them, you notice them everywhere in China. In this episode, Alastair explores the art of dragons, to understand how this mythical creature has become so ingrained in Chinese national identity.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: Nigel Farage has accused parts of the British media of mounting a politically-motivated campaign against him after reports about his financial interests prompted an investigation by Parliament's Standards Commissioner. Gabriel Pogrund, editor of the Insight investigations team at The Sunday Times, outlines the reporting behind their story, while journalist Michael Crick and Brendan O'Neill of Spiked discuss the political and journalistic issues. A High Court judgment has dismissed all claims brought by Prince Harry and six other claimants against Associated Newspapers over allegations that journalists used unlawful methods to obtain information. The ruling marks a significant moment in the long-running legal and political legacy of Britain's phone-hacking and press intrusion scandals. Susan Aslan, media lawyer, outlines the legal significance of the judgment.

programme.title: Talking Business
programme.desc: Is the space economy the next gold rush? Elon Musk's SpaceX has sparked the public's imagination after its recent stock market debut, but what will all the money being thrown into the space industry actually achieve? The founder of leading investment firm Space Capital, Chad Anderson, explains how what happens in orbit is increasingly important to the economy down on Earth. Ben Thompson also hears from the head of the UN's Office for Outer Space Affairs, Aarti Holla-Maini, about how space law is keeping up with an industry that is moving at breakneck speed. Also on the programme, the billionaire founder of New Zealand-based Rocket Lab, Sir Peter Beck, shines a light on what the private sector can achieve in space and whether SpaceX are friends or rivals.

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: Join the City Church, Cardiff, for their International Sunday Service, a weekly gathering that brings together people from diverse cultures for worship, biblical teaching and reflection.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roll up, roll up as Danny Sebastian takes us on a white-knuckle ride through the Bargain Hunt archive, showcasing all the fun of the fair. On a visit to the Scarborough Fair Collection, Danny remembers fairground and circus-related items bought by teams over the years, and counts down the most profitable at auction. Along the way, he learns about the fascinating history of the ghost train and how the words are put inside sticks of Blackpool rock.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher is in London to meet world-renowned adventurer, TV star and best-selling author Bear Grylls, who reflects upon his faith and favourite hymns.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the 2026 Wimbledon men's singles final.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: In the heart of Asia lie vast, arid lands. These deserts and dry grasslands are so hostile that they push life to the limit. Here, one of the rarest animals on earth, the Gobi bear, searches desperately for water, and the bizarre-looking long-eared jerboas use their striking features to catch prey. Asiatic lions must do whatever it takes to find food, and juvenile cormorants resort to cannibalism to survive. On the arid grasslands, Pallas cats need ingenious ways to get close to their prey, Mongolian gazelles must battle harsh winters and human barriers, and takhi are terrorised each night by Mongolian wolves. Life in the arid heart of Asia demands extraordinary resilience – those that live here are the continent's great survivors.

programme.title: I, Jack Wright
programme.desc: After a business tycoon's death, his wife Sally faces devastating news about his will. As mourners gather at their estate for the funeral, DCI Morgan uncovers a disturbing truth.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: I, Jack Wright
programme.desc: DCI Morgan investigates the death of Jack Wright. Sally, his third wife, challenges the will. Gray is pursued by a loan shark. Rose’s partner reveals her shocking news.

programme.title: The Notebook
programme.desc: In the run-up to the Second World War, a young couple's relationship falls foul of class prejudice.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben and Kate celebrate 25 years of friendship, animal stories and unforgettable moments on Animal Park, and revisit the extraordinary life of lioness Malaika.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A reclusive millionaire is found murdered in his home in west London, and police find hundreds of thousands of pounds in cash there. Why haven’t his killers taken the money? Only old-fashioned detective work can find them, but they’ve fled abroad. Also, a heavily pregnant woman is kidnapped and assaulted, but it’s not until the offender is arrested elsewhere for a lesser crime that police are able to connect him.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: Forensic explosives experts are tasked with examining the crime scenes after the London bombings to establish if the devices had the signature of any known terrorist organisation.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A tiger in a north London maisonette has Owain earning his stripes. Don’t panic, it’s not a real one! Meanwhile, Dion is dealing with damp in Kidsgrove, and Martel is in Hartlepool, where a two-bedroom mid-terrace has an intriguingly low guide price.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: It’s chocks away as Christina Trevanion pilots a special episode all about aviation. Among the historic aircraft of the Royal Airforce Museum London, Christina rounds up some deals that really took off at auction and others that had a bumpy ride. In Ardingly, a former flying ace and his daughter plan to soar to victory when they go shopping with Nick Hall, while in Newark, university friends Joel and Oscar hope to land a good deal with a propeller made into a coat rack, until their expert Catherine Southon grounds the idea. In Hendon, Christina learns about the history and heroism of the pilots who flew some of the 74 aircraft on display, and in Bristol, Eric Knowles reminisces about the glamour of supersonic travel from the luxurious interior of a surviving Concorde jet. Meanwhile, Natasha Raskin Sharp is flying high in a far older plane – a restored World War II Spitfire.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: A serious bike crash halts rush hour traffic, coastguards respond to reports of swimmers struggling in strong currents, recruits face strict drill checks, and travellers deal with lost luggage.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein kicks off his nearly 5,000-kilometre road odyssey by exploring Sydney, the city where he first arrived in Australia as a 19-year-old in the 1960s.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in north east Wales, helping a young couple hoping to find a slice of countryside heaven with room for their two children, a dog, veg patches and some chickens. From an eco-friendly modern home to a hillside mystery house with spectacular views, there’s plenty to explore. Alistair also meets a third-generation farmer who believes healthy soil is the main ingredient for a happy and sustainable future.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Jan’s past is packed with precious memories, but it’s time to cash in on keepsakes for her granddaughter’s future. Can the team turn love into loot when the gavel goes down?

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Lucy Porter. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Ethan Loch from Scotland is a talented pianist who has been blind since birth. Having secured a concert as soloist with The Royal Scottish National Orchestra and with hopes of becoming a full-time professional pianist and composer, Ethan and his family contemplate whether he needs to move to London to progress in the music industry.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian battles with a decision, Max makes an offer, and the Fox-Truemans prepare to welcome Denise home.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For decades, Dubai has sold itself as a safe place to live, do business and party. But when war broke out in nearby Iran, the city’s carefully curated image was shattered. Missiles and drones hit luxury hotels and critical infrastructure, expats scrambled for safety, and the government clamped down on sharing images of the war. Panorama reveals through the eyes of British residents, business owners and experts how Dubai’s promise of security and prosperity has been tested like never before.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps ex-England international, and football hard man Neil ‘Razor’ Ruddock find his softer side as he and his wife Leah escape to the country in rural Kent. Razor’s tough-guy persona is put to the test when a 15th-century thatched cottage causes him concern about spiders, but the former pro shows he is still as sharp as ever in a house hunt full of wit and warmth. Leah has aspirations to rear some animals in their new country pad, so Nicki takes the couple to a micro animal park to meet a selection of adorable farmyard friends, including a donkey that likes to dress up and a piano-playing pig!

programme.title: I, Jack Wright
programme.desc: John invites Emily to the business. Georgia puts pressure on the legal challenge to the will. Sally wants Jack's body exhumed, and DCI Morgan investigates further.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: I, Jack Wright
programme.desc: As Sally's legal challenge runs into trouble, her friendship with Arnaud takes a darker turn. With the court date approaching, Rose and Bobby come to a momentous decision.

programme.title: Cowboys & Aliens
programme.desc: Jake Lonergan wakes up in the desert with no memory of who he is or how he got there. But in the nearby town of Absolution, he's a wanted man, and Jake is arrested for being one of the most feared gunslingers in the Wild West, and the leader of the Lonergan gang. When the town comes under attack by mysterious craft that kidnap people off the streets, including the sheriff, the tough and feared Colonel Dolarhyde puts together a posse to find all the victims, including his kidnapped son. Mistrusted and feared by the others, Lonergan joins the posse and sets out to get justice.

programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Maned wolves arrive at the park for the first time. Before they can meet their future capybara and tapir housemates, the keepers must test their reactions.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: When a man dies in a house fire, it looks like a tragic accident. But two years later, it emerges he had a visitor before the fire. And his card was used to buy a flight to Italy. After all this time, will the airline be able to provide information about the passenger? In another case, police investigating a series of distraction burglaries follow a car to Northern Ireland, where an undercover cop is able to identify their man. And then, they bump into him in an unlikely location.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A blood spatter analyst helps put the killers of a 16-year-old behind bars, and a forensic speech expert matches a voice on police dashcam to the killer of PC Ian Broadhurst.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy is battling with fungus growing in a bungalow in Clacton. Jacqui is on the Isle of White, where a terrace is in need of resurrecting. Dion can’t move for all the junk inside a Cambrian mid-terrace.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and experts Richard Madley and Roo Irvine guide the teams around the Ripley Antiques Fair in North Yorkshire. Natasha also learns about one of the greenest forms of public transport, which is a lot older than you might think.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: It’s a busy holiday weekend in Bermuda, and PCs Holly Lavigne-Smith and Raeshaun 'Robbie' Robinson start their shift with what looks like a routine traffic stop. But a faulty brake light quickly leads to a more delicate situation, one that calls for calm decisions and a careful balance between enforcement and compassion. When they get a request to track down a lost wallet, it sounds straightforward, but it soon sends the pair across the city on a trail that doesn’t quite add up. Elsewhere, motorcycle officers PC Rajiv Bailey and PC Deforest Evans, backed up by PC Jason Ming, stop a visiting rider whose borrowed bike raises red flags. Multiple offences come to light, and the outcome leaves the visitor facing a long list of fines and a long walk home. Later, the officers' unexpected goodwill sees the man's luck changing. Out on the water, Coastguard Officer Hugo Benziger patrols with Private Edward Richardson and Corporal Gary Dowling Jr as holiday boat traffic fills the channels. Missing safety gear, busy raft-ups and high winds and rain keep the team alert, and even a lighthearted mishap doesn’t slow the patrol for long.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein heads north out of Sydney and explores the Hawkesbury River on the city’s fringe – a region defined by both the water and the bush.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in the Gloucestershire countryside, helping Charlotte from Croydon in south London find a rural escape with help from her eldest son, Ben. Included in the fantastic mix of properties are character cottages, a spacious new build and an imaginative conversion, offering plenty to tempt her. Taking a break from house hunting, Denise then heads to a local farm to meet some adorable spring lambs before getting stuck into feeding time.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Auctioneer James Broad and restorer Saf Fakir travel to Manchester to help Caroline, whose life was turned upside down when her husband Nick was diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer’s. Now in a care home, Nick’s absence has left Caroline alone in the family home they built together, surrounded by a lifetime of memories. With her sister-in-law Heather by her side, she’s now ready to face the emotional challenge of sorting through Nick's collections. JB uncovers family heirlooms and fascinating finds, from vintage toys and enamel signs to a remarkable collection of sketches by celebrated Manchester artist Harry Rutherford. Meanwhile, Saf works his magic on a well-loved bamboo hall stand and a broken vintage sign that needs some TLC. He’s determined to restore them to their former glory. Every item sold brings Caroline a step closer to easing the cost of Nick’s ongoing care and to finding peace as she begins a new chapter of her own.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gyles Brandreth. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Laura Davies faces her biggest challenge yet - flying her late husband's hot-air balloon at a festival held in his memory and battling grief to keep the family legacy alive.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise struggles to settle back into family life, it’s the day of Ian’s plea hearing, and Mark raises suspicions.

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The Great British Sewing Bee returns with brand new host Sophie Willan. Over ten weeks, 12 of the country’s best home sewers compete to be crowned winner of the Sewing Bee crown. In the opening week, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young set three challenges on the theme of the British summer. First up, the Pattern Challenge sees them crafting summer shorts with a patch pocket. Next, in the 90-minute Transformation Challenge, barbecue aprons become bags. Finally, there is a Sewing Bee first in the Made-to-Measure challenge as the sewers are asked to create garments to fit themselves. They become their own models as they make outfits for their ideal summer’s day, and once complete, the catwalk is all theirs. One sewer will impress and earn Garment of the Week, but for another, it’s the end of the road as they leave the sewing room for good.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When a woman reports a murder and is then found strangled, the team are left wondering whether she was reporting her own murder.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
programme.desc: Despite an Avengers encounter, Peter Parker’s amazing abilities and Tony Stark-supplied costume are stuck serving only his own New York neighbourhood. Then an encounter with crooks using alien tech, led by the cynical Vulture, draws him into a dangerous adventure – almost as perilous as wanting to take the girl of his dreams to their high school homecoming dance.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick tries to prove he is not superstitious to the padre, while Simon tries to earn respect by joining in dangerous homemade games with Mac and Rocket.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick and Simon help each other to learn to dance, whilst the arrival of vet Parikh reveals Bird as a terrible flirt.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Comedy series about a British bomb disposal detachment in Afghanistan. A mortar attack on the patrol base sends Bluestone 42 diving for cover.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: Captured by Vikings, the Doctor and Clara must help protect their village from space warriors from the future, the Mire. Outnumbered and outgunned, their fate seems inevitable. So why is the Doctor preoccupied with a single Viking girl?

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: England, 1651. Deadly highwayman 'the Knightmare' and his sidekick stalk the dark streets of London. But when they find loot that is not of this world, they come face to face with the Doctor. Who is the Knightmare in league with? And can the Doctor avoid the hangman's noose and protect the Earth from a devilish betrayal?

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey attempts to clear Billy’s name. Kim continues to avoid Denise out of guilt.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea completely unravels. Avani tries to reach out to Ravi.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya finds herself trapped. Jack pushes Kim to visit Denise. Kojo asks Harry and Gina to help him find a girlfriend.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max scrambles to excuse his behaviour. Priya receives a shock. Kim and Howie confront Sheila.

programme.title: Elvis
programme.desc: An epic, big-screen spectacle from Baz Luhrmann that explores the life and music of Elvis Presley. In a thoroughly cinematic drama, Elvis's story is seen through the lens of his complicated relationship with his enigmatic manager, Colonel Tom Parker. As told by Parker, the film delves into the tangled dynamic between the two, spanning over 20 years, from Presley's rise to fame to his unprecedented stardom, against the backdrop of the evolving cultural landscape.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex vanished on holiday with his mother and grandfather for six years. Now, he's retracing his steps across Europe to tell the full story for the first time.

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: Chloe Ayling is kidnapped at a fake modelling shoot in Milan. When the police learn she’s due to be auctioned as a sex slave, a major investigation begins.

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: Kidnapped and held in a remote farmhouse, and with Black Death’s auction looming, Chloe realises she must find a way to gain control over her captor.

programme.title: Chloe Ayling: My Unbelievable Kidnapping
programme.desc: Glamour model and kidnapping victim Chloe Ayling continues to look back at her terrifying ordeal orchestrated by Black Death. Now safely under the protection of the Italian authorities, she must give evidence in court against the man who enabled her escape from the international sex-trafficking gang – Lukasz Herba. But as the hearing starts and the evidence is laid out, a different picture begins to emerge of this particular suspect. Dazed from the shock of this discovery, Chloe returns home to the UK, finally free of the spectre of a gang she believed could continue to threaten her life. Whilst now free from one unimaginable ordeal, she’s immediately plunged into another as the Italian courts publicly release details of the case and the ongoing investigation to the world’s press. What she hoped to bury as an embarrassing nightmare soon ignites the interest of the global media. Now they know her name and discover where she lives.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Five stand-up hopefuls battle it out at the York Hall in Bethnal Green to earn a place in the grand final, where one will be crowned the BBC New Comedian 2023.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: Studious Peter Parker accidentally gains superpowers, but it takes a tragedy to teach him how to be a hero and the price of keeping secrets.

programme.title: Spider-Man 2
programme.desc: Peter Parker’s double life takes its toll, and his superhero powers fail just as New York faces a new threat: crazed genius Dr Octavius and his four robotic arms building a destructive fusion reactor.

programme.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
programme.desc: This film looks at the rivalry between Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, which transcended clubs, countries and competitions for over a decade, to become the defining story of modern football.

programme.title: England 2006: The Golden Generation
programme.desc: With Beckham, Rooney, Ferdinand and Cole on the team, the 2006 World Cup was supposed to be England's moment. So why did the dream end in on-field agony and off-field acrimony?

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive England's controversial exit at the 2010 World Cup.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara meets Isobel, a young mum stalked by an ex-partner, and Jen, whose stalker, a stranger, is in prison for the crime but has just been given a release date.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara follows Laura, stalked by a controlling ex, and Liv, haunted by an anonymous cyberstalker for six years, as both battle to be believed and protected.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive a hugely dramatic World Cup quarter-final at Lusail Stadium.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Southern Spain, famous for its beaches and sunshine, has become the main gateway for drugs into Europe. Violent turf wars between drug cartels have caused the government to issue a crackdown. For the last two years, the police have been fighting to take back control. In this compelling film, Stacey gains unique access to the police, the dealers and the smugglers. She goes out on patrol with the air force team of the Guardia Civil and is invited along to witness a night-time raid in an attempt to arrest a cartel suspect they have been watching for two years. To understand why this part of Spain sees so much cocaine, Stacey travels to the source – Apartado, Colombia. There she meets one of the biggest smugglers in the region. Faced with highly organised criminal gangs and the insatiable demand for drugs in Europe, Stacey debates whether this a war the police can ever win. Part of the award-winning This World strand.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Spider-Man 3
programme.desc: Peter Parker faces multiple threats when former friend Harry seeks revenge, an alien organism wants a host, a killer is transformed and his beloved Mary Jane has a change of heart.

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: After the party the night before, one girl wakes up wrangling with her heart. With the final Kiss-Off just around the corner, the arrival of loved ones offers some timely advice. But will the divas decide to turn for their partner and commit to a relationship back home or call time on their summer of love? Strap in for a rollercoaster ride that will leave you laughing and crying as the masseria sprinkles its magic for one last time.

programme.title: I Kissed a Girl
programme.desc: Fasten your sequins, fly your flags, raise a toast and get ready for the mother of all kikis where everyone’s invited. The ultimate cheer squad is here to celebrate four unforgettable loud and proud summers of love of the UK’s first ever queer dating show. Take a front row seat with queen of the masseria Dannii Minogue, Charley Marlowe and Layton Williams for the ultimate I Kissed a Girl and I Kissed a Boy mash-up.

programme.title: Cowboys & Aliens
programme.desc: Jake Lonergan wakes up in the desert with no memory of who he is or how he got there. But in the nearby town of Absolution, he's a wanted man, and Jake is arrested for being one of the most feared gunslingers in the Wild West, and the leader of the Lonergan gang. When the town comes under attack by mysterious craft that kidnap people off the streets, including the sheriff, the tough and feared Colonel Dolarhyde puts together a posse to find all the victims, including his kidnapped son. Mistrusted and feared by the others, Lonergan joins the posse and sets out to get justice.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th birthday of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern half-a-million pound interpretation of this classic sports car before putting on a typically low-key birthday party involving live music, classic fighter planes and a group of Royal Marines. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond is in South Africa to test a bigger, even more rugged alternative to the famous Hummer, while James May meets up with gold medal winning British Olympian Amy Williams for a wintry test of the latest Mini rally car. Plus the power crazed BMW 1 series M is on the test track and another top celebrity guest takes their place in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights of the fifth game of the T20 series between England and India.

programme.title: Queen James: Passion, Plots and Power in the Court of Britain’s First King
programme.desc: Gareth Russell reveals the story of King James VI and I, his fondness for male favourites and a scandalous murder trial that threatened to blow his monarchy apart.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: A full rerun of the Norway v England quarter-final from Miami Stadium, Florida, at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani begins a four-part journey along England’s east coast, exploring the wildlife, landscapes and communities adapting to life on the edge of the sea. Her first stop is Northey Island in Essex, an internationally important refuge for threatened birds, including curlew, lapwing and dunlin. With rising sea levels putting increasing pressure on coastal habitats, Anita discovers an innovative conservation project using retired Thames barges to help protect the island’s future. She also meets the volunteers working to safeguard vulnerable ringed plover nests using a simple but effective solution. Anita also delves into the Countryfile archives to revisit some of the projects helping to create more space for nature to thrive.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway revisits three wildlife walks. First, he braves the rain in Gwynedd, then hunts for water voles in Radnorshire and finally sees ancient rock formations in Ceredigion.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: George Anderson and Calum Clunie are back together to tackle the week’s jobs at Beechgrove Garden. Although George got a special picking of potatoes for his birthday in June, this is the time to lift the first crop from the vegetable plot and try out this year’s produce. Calum and George check on the brassicas that were planted in the club root-infected bed, testing varieties that are said to be resistant to the infection. George is back to work on the privet hedge that was given a severe pruning last year to encourage thicker growth, Calum gets round to placing plants known for their fragrance in his competition bed, and there are the week’s handy hints.

programme.title: This Wild Life
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Saba Douglas-Hamilton, together with her husband Frank and their three young children, move to the romantic Samburu National Reserve in Kenya to run a safari camp for the first time. But life out there isn't easy. There are monkeys in the kitchen, floods threatening the camp and, with no fences to keep out the wildlife, elephants are regular house guests. This series follows their ups and downs as the family struggles to keep guests, elephants, lions and even crocodiles smiling. The family is settling into their new life running a safari camp and protecting elephants in northern Kenya's remote Samburu National Reserve. Guests have arrived and are expecting a luxury wildlife experience, but Saba is called away to rescue an injured crocodile. And the camp team comes face to face with a bull elephant in a dramatic search for a young child lost in the bush. It's a nail-biting adventure for Saba and her warrior friends.

programme.title: Talking Tennis
programme.desc: Tennis addict Alistair McGowan serves up an affectionate look back at one of the world's greatest tournaments - Wimbledon. Here, he is focusing on the game's greatest rivalries and the big battles - physical and mental - that have been fought over the years on the lawns of SW19 and helped turn two weeks of British summertime into our favourite gladiatorial spectacle. Featuring classic interviews and rarely seen footage from the BBC's archives, this episode examines the confrontations between Evert and Navratilova, Ashe and Connors, Borg and McEnroe, Graf and Seles, and Sampras and Agassi - as well as the more recent rivalries between the Williams sisters, Federer, Nadal and Djokovik. It also looks at the tactics players can use to triumph as they push each other to the limit - mind games, tantrums and the fine art of grunting.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Andi Oliver’s Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: In her old neighbourhood of Notting Hill, in west London, Andi Oliver revisits the past with her daughter Miquita to create her most fabulous feast yet, celebrating those doing incredible work to uplift the community. When she was in her early 20s, Andi raised her daughter in W10, and she wants to see if the community spirit she remembers from back in the day still exists here. For Andi and her daughter, it’s an emotional return to the familiar streets, meeting individuals working tirelessly to help those who need support. If she’s to do them justice, Andi needs to create an unforgettable feast that draws on the cultures that had a huge impact on her time here, particularly from the Caribbean and Morocco. At least she has a spectacular setting in mind for her party – the iconic Grade II listed block Trellick Tower. Can Andi pull together the ultimate block party with incredible music and unforgettable food that does justice to the place she once called home?

programme.title: Sweet Charity
programme.desc: Musical comedy about the romantic ups and downs of a dance partner-for-hire at a seedy New York dance hall, who longs for true love but has bad luck with men.

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: The Bennet family of Longbourn are delighted to learn of the occupancy of nearby Netherfield Park by Charles Bingley, a wealthy young bachelor. Bingley develops an interest in Jane, the eldest of the five unmarried Bennet girls, but her sister Elizabeth is far from impressed by Bingley's haughty companion Mr Darcy.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of the best finds from the show's travels around the country.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The next stop on Anita's road trip is West Mersea in Essex. While the county may be known for engineering and car manufacturing, along its coastline one industry has a much longer history. Oysters have been harvested here for more than 2,000 years, cultivated in the creeks around Mersea Island. Over the decades, oyster farming has adapted to changing environmental conditions and the arrival of the non-native Pacific rock oyster. To experience what oyster harvesting is like today, Anita heads onto the brackish waters to meet the people farming oysters. She also meets the team investigating how the non-native Pacific rock oyster is affecting the local ecosystem and whether it could play a role in helping coastal habitats respond to rising sea levels. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the people and projects shaping life around our coastline.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow comes from the beautiful grounds of the imposing Elizabethan mansion Wollaton Hall in Nottingham. Silver expert Kate Flitcroft is shaken, not stirred, by a stunning fire extinguisher cocktail shaker that turns up, while ceramics expert Eric Knowles meets his match with a cheeky toy monkey popping out of a musical pineapple. Fiona Bruce arrives in style on an iconic Chopper bike and tells the story of Raleigh Bicycles, founded in Nottingham in the 1880s. She learns about local activist George Powe, who campaigned to secure employment opportunities for black workers at Raleigh in the 1950s, and hears from two former employees about life inside what was once the world’s largest bicycle factory. Jewellery expert Susan Rumfitt challenges Fiona to guess the value of three intriguing cameo items, and collectables expert Hilary Kay is surprised to discover some of Queen Victoria’s personal undergarments with a sizeable price tag to boot.

programme.title: The Tech Billionaire Takeover
programme.desc: A new generation of tech billionaires believe their technologies can replace democracy as we know it and upend the global financial system. Matt Shea gets inside access to this new super-elite, visiting the sovereign nations they're building and tracing their growing political connections to the Trump administration. Are these men trying to take over the world?

programme.title: Oppenheimer
programme.desc: The life of J Robert Oppenheimer, the physicist appointed by the US to run the Manhattan Project, their bid to develop the atomic bomb before the Nazis. Brilliant as he is, winning that race comes at a cost, and amidst his guilt and grief at what he's done, he is also suspected by the authorities of being a communist spy.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: A look back at the day’s play at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Golf: Scottish Open
programme.desc: Highlights from Sunday’s play as a stacked field featuring the world’s best players compete for the Genesis Scottish Open at The Renaissance Club in East Lothian. Masters Champion Rory McIlroy and World Number One Scottie Scheffler headline a stellar start list for the event which takes place a week before the 154th Open Championship. American Chris Gotterup is in great form as he looks to defend the title he won in 2025, with the home challengers including PGA Champion and 2020 Scottish Open winner Aaron Rai, 2024 winner Bob McIntyre, along with Tommy Fleetwood, Tyrrell Hatton, Marco Penge and the Fitzpatrick brothers.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: In Search of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges embarks on a journey to see if the beautiful game still exists. He meets Brazil football icon Cafu, Scottish legend John McGinn, Sao Paulo ultras, US football fans and many others.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: An expert witness uses fibre analysis to solve an execution-style murder, and after 26 years in prison, a man walks free when a forensic scientist links DNA to the real killer.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Viewers will be checking their change after watching this episode in Kent! David Harper meets a coin collector who reveals which rare 50 pence piece is worth 300 times its face value! John Wilson’s collection boasts 11,000 coins, including early medieval and Tudor hammered coins and the very first milled ones from the 18th century. Hoping to splash some cash of their own at Detling antiques fair are two teams of bargain hunters. In red are mum and daughter Nisha and dancer Adzi, with their expert Mark Stacey. Their opponents in blue are partners and musicians Kathy and Nardo. Nardo used to play drums in 1980s band Amazulu, and Kathy worked with some big recording artists of the era. They hope to compose a win with expert Kate Bliss, kicking off with a dolphin water feature, but four figures is too much to splash out on. The reds, meanwhile, are pinning their hopes on a set of cameo brooches, but Mark has doubts whether they will prove popular at auction. In Windsor, David hears auctioneer Harry Ballin’s estimates before the teams see their items go under the hammer – for a profit or a loss!

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restore a ceremonial Chinese lion dance costume, a set of well-loved traditional beer pumps and two beautiful ceramic maquettes.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents another semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are the Norman Conquest, Borussia Dortmund, popes of the Renaissance and the film musicals of Alan Menken.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sarah is a passionate livestock farmer and devoted to her Kunekune pigs. As she heads to the south east’s famous Edenbridge and Oxted Agricultural Show, she has high hopes of them walking away as rosette winners. Meanwhile, farming couple Frances and Simon are making their debut appearance at the show with their pedigree Dexter cattle, some of which play an important conservation role grazing in the South Downs.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom explores a part of the industry close to his heart: the highly skilled and competitive world of fine dining. He meets chefs who, like him, are obsessed with the pursuit of culinary excellence. Is it possible to reach perfection when chefs are, by nature, never satisfied?

programme.title: Yellowstone: Wildest Winter to Blazing Summer
programme.desc: Patrick follows the grizzly bears that are taking a risk with the weather by leaving their winter dens early. Hungry wolves are struggling to bring down their elk prey in the unusually shallow snow. And for great grey owls, it is the iciness of the snow that is hampering their hunts. Yellowstone's winter is always one of the most brutal on the planet. But 2016 saw weather records broken, and the wildlife was forced to adapt to survive. Kate Humble gets to grips with the science behind this remarkable season, from understanding the importance of the snowpack's structure as the melt begins to uncovering why Yellowstone's unique geology poses problems for some grazers' teeth.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Chiddingstone Castle in Kent. He visits Anne Boleyn's family home, Hever Castle, and reveals what influenced her rise to power. Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Adam Partridge unearth unique antiques and collectables that reflect the local Kent character.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: If you could do just one thing to transform your health, what should it be? Zoe Ball, Roman Kemp and Clive Myrie show how one small change can make a big difference. From dancing yourself happier to eating mackerel for your heart, to soaking in a hot bath to help you sleep – they share the simple and surprising things we could all do to improve our lives. In postcard-perfect Rye in East Sussex, busy mum Sam is on a mission to supercharge her health after shedding the pounds with the help of weight-loss jabs. Clive Myrie has a simple but powerful idea for her next step – eating oily fish. And it turns out that adding this one food to your diet packs a serious health punch. Omega-3-rich heroes like mackerel, salmon and sardines won’t just help keep the weight off – they could sharpen mental focus, calm inflammation and even lower the risk of heart attacks and strokes.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the first heat of the classic quiz where contenders face the famous black chair with the ultimate goal of becoming the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are 15th-century king Edward IV, the band Depeche Mode, the films of Yorgos Lanthimos and Roy Keane.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the quiz where knowledge only gets you so far – as always, the vital extra ingredients are logic and lateral thinking. Three rowers take on a team of microbiologists in finding connections between apparently random clues. For example: Germany, HM Treasury, the Galactic Republic and the Duchy of Lancaster. What connects them?

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The series kicks off with a match between students from Bristol and Imperial. Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: Chris Packham embarks on an extraordinary journey through deep time to uncover how a creature as vast, complex and charismatic as the elephant came to be. From a tiny cell over four billion years ago to one of the planet’s most iconic giants, this is the surprising story of life's most radical transformation.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob accept a special invitation to fish the estate lake at Burghley House, which sits on the border of Cambridgeshire and Lincolnshire. Built in the 16th century by William Cecil, Elizabeth I’s lord high chancellor, Burghley House is home to some of the most majestic landscapes Paul and Bob have ever found themselves in. With a spectacular lake and stunning gardens, all designed by the iconic Capability Brown, Paul and Bob hope they can do the place justice - and net a decent catch.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Safe Haven
programme.desc: A young woman with a mysterious past lands in Southport, North Carolina, where her bond with a widower forces her to confront the dark secret that haunts her.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani begins a four-part journey along England’s east coast, exploring the wildlife, landscapes and communities adapting to life on the edge of the sea. Her first stop is Northey Island in Essex, an internationally important refuge for threatened birds, including curlew, lapwing and dunlin. With rising sea levels putting increasing pressure on coastal habitats, Anita discovers an innovative conservation project using retired Thames barges to help protect the island’s future. She also meets the volunteers working to safeguard vulnerable ringed plover nests using a simple but effective solution. Anita also delves into the Countryfile archives to revisit some of the projects helping to create more space for nature to thrive.

programme.title: Why...?
programme.desc: During intense storms on 19 August 2024, the 54m superyacht Bayesian mysteriously sinks off the coast of Sicily. Seven people onboard tragically perish. As search and rescue teams scour the coast for survivors, questions begin to be asked. Why did a multi-million-pound superyacht sink when others nearby didn’t? Could its record-breaking mast have made it more vulnerable to strong winds? This film forensically examines what went wrong and asks whether more needs to be done to keep ships safe at sea. Eyewitnesses, first responders, a former captain of the Bayesian and a retired MAIB principal investigator detail what happened.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay returns for the penultimate day of this week’s quiz as the trip heads to Kirkby Lonsdale. With the leaderboard tightening, presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peter’s Abby Cook are all vying for victory. Paul Gorton resumes his role as unreliable tour guide, scattering facts and fabrications as the competition edges closer to the finale.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Jan’s past is packed with precious memories, but it’s time to cash in on keepsakes for her granddaughter’s future. Can the team turn love into loot when the gavel goes down?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in north east Wales, helping a young couple hoping to find a slice of countryside heaven with room for their two children, a dog, veg patches and some chickens. From an eco-friendly modern home to a hillside mystery house with spectacular views, there’s plenty to explore. Alistair also meets a third-generation farmer who believes healthy soil is the main ingredient for a happy and sustainable future.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Police in Hertfordshire respond to an emergency that uncovers a shocking crime, Scottish fire crews battle a wildfire visible from space, and Paul Colgan joins farm safety training.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: In this what-happened-next special, the team search again for a daughter who cares for her mum to try to find the second ring she lost while wild swimming. Archive detective Sam has one more go at cracking the case of a camera filled with memories that so far no-one has claimed. Finally, a few special moments for people from the series after the cameras stopped.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall, Jodie Kidd, Jamali Maddix and Suzi Perry take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the third semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win a place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are the PDC World Darts Championship, the history of transportation to Australia, Parks and Recreation, and Queen Anne.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Derbyshire duo Becky and Matt hold down second jobs to fund their dream of rearing their flock of pedigree Dutch spotted sheep. Winning rosettes at the Royal Cheshire County Show is recognition for breeding the best, so Becky is going all out for a prize even though it’s her first time competing here. Joining them in the show ring with their own Dutch spotted contenders are farming couple Sharron and Peter, who live in Yorkshire. They have been passionate promoters of this breed from the get-go, so a win for them will mean their hard work has been worth it.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Tom continues his deep dive into the industry he knows and loves. This time, he meets brave and ambitious entrepreneurs looking to expand their businesses in the midst of some of the toughest economic conditions the industry has ever seen. In the high-risk game of hospitality, when do you twist, stick or fold?

programme.title: Yellowstone: Wildest Winter to Blazing Summer
programme.desc: The story of animals surviving one of the harshest seasonal changes on the planet continues. As winter turns to spring, temperatures rise and Yellowstone bursts into life. Beavers feast on new green shoots, baby bison take their first faltering steps and grizzly bear cubs emerge from dens to explore this new green world. But spring is also a perilous time. Wolves are hungrier than usual after slim winter pickings. And rising temperatures melt the vast mountain snow pack, sending a million tons of water flooding into the rivers. Only the toughest will survive.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode revisits some of the locations from the series, with never-seen-before valuations. It's a special show touching down at the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum, Croombe in south Worcestershire and Crowcombe Court in Somerset. We find some fascinating items to take to auction, and Paul Martin discovers a hidden pocket of Cornwall and the historic estate of Mount Edgcumbe.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Eniola Aluko, Phil Daniels, Nikita Kanda and Danny Miller take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Sheffield, empty nesters Siva and Dhami are feeling stuck in a rut. But Roman Kemp is about to shake things up. He wants to show them how taking up dancing could do far more than just put a spring in their step. Research reveals that adding dance to your weekly routine can boost fitness, increase brain power and lift your mood. A little rhythm might just rewrite Siva and Dhami's whole routine.

programme.title: Rick Stein’s Food Stories
programme.desc: To end his UK food tour, Rick goes night fishing in Wales and meets the farmer dodging WW2 bombs to raise salt marsh lamb. He visits the spot where Dylan Thomas wrote Under Milk Wood, then heads to Bristol for the ultimate jerk chicken. In the West Country, he meets a pioneer of the organic food movement, a famous female cheesemaker, and chef Mark Hix, who cooks him pollock, cockles and seashore veg.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the west of the UK, a spectacular monument older than Stonehenge, a 200-year-old mine trapped in time and a lost medieval friary.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig celebrates the hundredth birthday of the BBC with Zoe Lyons, Greg James, Richard Osman and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The magnificent Jen Brister introduces comedians Esther Manito and Adam Rowe to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O’Connell.

programme.title: ODI Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights from the first ODI of 2026 between England and India.

programme.title: On the Basis of Sex
programme.desc: The story of a young Ruth Bader Ginsburg – then a struggling attorney and new mother – who faces adversity and numerous obstacles in her fight for equal rights throughout her career. When Ruth takes on a groundbreaking tax case with her husband, attorney Martin Ginsburg, she knows it could change the direction of her career and the way the courts view gender discrimination. The film also chronicles the romance between Ruth and Marty, a partnership that succeeded both personally and professionally.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When Boy, Girl and Cheese learn that their animal siblings have a tough time going through life with their unwieldy tails, they want to understand what it's like. So Mouse builds them mechanical tails to wear, and the three soon learn just how hard it is to do simple tasks. However, they come to master their tails well enough to attend the Curling Tail festival, where all tailed animals celebrate themselves. But when the kids turn their tails to full power to fit in better, they go haywire! They're now in danger of ruining the festival and being discovered as frauds!

programme.title: Cooking Buddies
programme.desc: Get your dancing shoes ready because Buddy and Conker get a call from Jaylen, a young salsa dancer who doesn’t stop moving! His mum is a professional salsa dancer, and Jaylen has learned all his moves from her, as he loves connecting with their Colombian roots through dancing. But there’s one thing that Jaylen loves even more... eggs! He asks Buddy to teach him an egg-cellent meal that he can make for his family. Buddy knows that if Jaylen loves dancing salsa, then he has to get him making salsa too, so he comes up with a cracking egg tortilla wrap filled with guacamole and zingy salsa. They learn to make their own tortilla dough in minutes, an omelette that sticks to the tortilla in the pan and colourful tomato salsa and creamy guacamole – cracking! Jaylen’s keen to get Buddy learning his kind of salsa too, so Buddy learns the basics of posture and footwork with the help of Jaylen… and some colourful socks! Join Buddy, Conker and Jaylen in this episode of Cooking Buddies that gets salsa onto your plate and into your step. You can get all of Buddy’s recipes, and loads more, at bbc.co.uk/food

When animal costumes turn up at their door, Joe and Adam know another messy, wild challenge from Little Bro Callum’s about to begin. Can Adam’s dung beetle take on Joe’s kangaroo and win? programme.desc: Steve's on a mission to find dinosaurs that have something a little special... fearsome killers, with incredible abilities! Get ready for a sensory sensation! His quest sees him race in the dark like a troodon and track prey from miles away taking tips from the daspletosaurus, and finally he gets his hands on a real-life caiman to show how their pressure pits are just like that of the spinosaurus. Strap yourself in for a sensory overload!

programme.title: Horrible Science

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The One Zoo Three brothers are on a mission to compose a zoo musical using the sounds of the animals. Lar gibbons Shany and Raff swing into action and audition to be the perfect percussionists, while a health check with Bombura the loud kookaburra sees him in with a shout for lead vocals. Even playful jaguar cub Inka proves she can add a cracking big crunch to the mix! Meanwhile, Aaron reveals some facts about Darwin the hornbill’s hidden talents, Tyler takes on a breathtaking challenge with an anaconda, and the brothers recall their conservation travels to Uganda to see how rangers are helping protect the African elephant population.

programme.title: Gym Stars

programme.title: The Football Academy
